title: Sigmoidotropis ekmaniana
taxonomy: Sigmoidotropis ekmaniana is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is part of the genus Sigmoidotropis, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as horse's hooves. The classification of Sigmoidotropis ekmaniana is further defined as a member of the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its compound leaves and pinnate venation. Within the subfamily, Sigmoidotropis ekmaniana is thought to be closely related to other species in the same genus.

intro: Sigmoidotropis ekmaniana, also known as the Cuban horse bean, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, native to the Caribbean and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Sigmoidotropis ekmaniana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
